title: On Seeing The Apollo Belvidere
content:
What majesty! what elegance and grace!
    The form how perfect! how divine the face!
    In admiration rapt, I gazing stand:
    Is this a statue wrought by mortal hand?
    No! 'tis Apollo's self, methinks I see;
    I feel the presence of the Deity.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Oldham, titled "On Seeing The Apollo Belvidere", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
